vehicleProfile.notes: 6.2L L92 V8 configuration with no Active Fuel Management hardware. Full-size Denali XL AWD; verify axle ratio, rear differential option, and RPO codes before ordering axle-specific parts.

components.oilSpecification: SAE 5W-30 full synthetic meeting current GM dexos or API requirements
components.engineOilCapacityWithFilter: Approximately 6 qt; confirm final dipstick level
components.oilFilter: ACDelco PF48/PF63 family depending on current service supersession; verify by VIN
components.brakeFluid: DOT 3 brake fluid meeting GM specification
components.transferCase: Full-time AWD transfer case; verify exact transfer-case model and fluid specification by RPO/build information
components.rearDifferential: Confirm axle and differential option by RPO code before ordering fluid additives, cover gasket, or internal parts
components.tireLugNutTorque: 140 lb-ft; verify against current owner's manual

services.name: Engine Oil & Filter Change
tools: Drain pan rated for at least 8 qt
materials: Approximately 6 qt SAE 5W-30 full-synthetic engine oil
No drain-plug washer unless the installed plug uses one
steps: Warm the engine briefly, park level, set the parking brake, and let oil cool enough to work safely.
Remove the 15 mm oil-pan drain plug and drain oil fully.
Remove the filter, confirming the old gasket comes off with it.
Lubricate the new filter gasket with clean oil and install hand-tight per the filter instructions.
Install the drain plug and torque it to the current factory specification.
Refill with approximately 6 qt of 5W-30, start the engine, inspect for leaks, and set final level on the dipstick.
Reset Oil Life Monitor with ignition on and engine off by pressing the accelerator pedal three times within five seconds, if that procedure matches the cluster configuration.
services.tips: Your L92 is non-AFM. Do not add AFM-lifter monitoring or AFM-disabler procedures that belong to 5.3L or other AFM-equipped GM engines.
services.whatItDoes: Replaces engine oil and filter to protect the L92 V8's bearings, camshaft, timing system, and valvetrain.
services.whyItMatters: Regular oil service is the most important routine maintenance item for long-term V8 durability.
